CALENDARIA.ClimateEditor.Presets.Instruc: Override weather behavior per-zone per-season. Use the Season dropdown to select which season to edit. Weight sets a per-season override: an absolute number replaces the season base weight, a number with + or - suffix adjusts it relatively (e.g., 8+ adds 8, 3- subtracts 3), 0 force-excludes the preset, and empty means no override. The placeholder shows the zone base weight for reference. Temp Min/Max override the zone's base temperature when this weather is active; use a number suffix + or - for relative adjustments (e.g., 5+ = 5° warmer than base). Inertia controls how long weather persists before changing.
CALENDARIA.ClimateEditor.Presets.NameToo: Custom display name for this weather type in this zone. Leave blank to use the default name.

CALENDARIA.ClimateEditor.Presets.WeightT: Per-season weight override. Absolute number replaces the season base, N+ or N- adjusts relatively, 0 excludes, empty = no override.
CALENDARIA.ClimateEditor.Presets.ZeroWei: This preset is enabled but its effective weight is zero, so it will never be selected. Set a weight above 0 here or on the season climate.

CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Cycles: Named repeating patterns like zodiac signs or elemental weeks.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.CycleVariance: How much cycle length varies between cycles. 0 = consistent, 1 = highly erratic (cycle length can range from 0% to 200% of base).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.DaylightEnabled: When enabled, sunrise and sunset times vary throughout the year based on the shortest and longest day configured below. When disabled, daylight is a static 50/50 split.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.DaylightZoneOverr: Climate zones with a latitude or manual daylight setting will override these global values.

CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.DaysPerYearComput: Calculated from the sum of days in all months.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Eclipse: Configure how often this moon produces solar and lunar eclipses.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.EclipseMode: Rare produces 1–2 eclipses per year, Occasional 3–4, and Frequent 6–8. Custom lets you define the nodal period directly.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.FirstWeekday: Which weekday falls on the first day of year 0.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.HoursPerDay: Number of hours in each day.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LeapDays: Days in this month during leap years (leave empty if same).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LeapGregorian: Uses Gregorian rules: divisible by 400, or by 4 but not 100. (e.g., 2000 and 2004 are leap years, 1900 is not)
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LeapInterval: How many years between leap years (e.g., 4 for every 4 years).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LeapPattern: Comma-separated intervals. Use ! prefix to exclude. Example: "400,!100,4" = Gregorian rules.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LeapStart: The first year that is a leap year (offset for calculations).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LongestDay: Hours of daylight on the longest day.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.LuxonSync: Align this calendar with the system WorldClock. Requires a Gregorian-shape calendar.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Months: Define the months of your calendar. Order matters - drag to reorder.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.MoonBrightness: How much this moon affects nighttime scene lighting.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.MoonBrightnessMax: How much this moon brightens nighttime scenes when full. 0 = no effect, 0.15 = default, 0.3 = maximum.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Moons: Configure moons and their cycles. Each moon has a reference date when it was new.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Name: This name will appear in the calendar selector.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.NamedWeeks: Named repeating week periods (e.g., 'Week of the Harvest', 'Holy Week').
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.NamedYears: Assign display names to specific years. Use [yearName|YYYY] in format strings.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.NodalPeriod: Days for the eclipse season cycle to repeat. Lower values produce more frequent eclipses.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.PhaseMode: Fixed moons follow a regular cycle. Randomized moons have erratic cycles controlled by a seed, with optional anchor dates that guarantee a specific phase.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.PhaseSeed: Seed value for deterministic random phase generation. Same seed always produces the same phases. Use the dice button to randomize.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.ReferenceDate: A known date and phase used to anchor moon cycle calculations.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.ResetWeekdays: Start each year on the first weekday, resetting the continuous weekday cycle.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.SeasonalType: Required for equinox and solstice anchors and weather integrations. Without it, astronomical anchors will not resolve for this season.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.SeasonOffset: Day of year when the first season begins (0 = first day of year).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Seasons: Define seasons by day ranges. Optional.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.SeasonsFields: Each row: name, abbreviation, icon (FontAwesome class), color, and date range (start month/day to end month/day).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.SeasonsFieldsPeri: Each row: name, abbreviation, icon (FontAwesome class), color, and duration in days.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.SeasonType: Dated seasons have specific start/end dates. Periodic seasons repeat based on duration.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.ShortestDay: Hours of daylight on the shortest day.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.SummerSolstice: Date when daylight is longest (summer solstice).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.System: The game world or setting this calendar is from.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Template: Load an existing calendar as a starting point for customization.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.TimeStructure: Configure how time is measured in your world.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.Weekdays: Define the days of the week. The week repeats in this order.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.WeeksPerMonth: For month-based counting, how many weeks fit in each month.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.WeeksRepeat: When enabled, named weeks repeat beyond the last defined week number. When disabled, only the explicitly numbered weeks are named.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.WeekStart: Display only. Rotates the calendar grid so this weekday appears first. Cycle order is unchanged.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.WeeksType: How named weeks progress. Per Year counts weeks continuously across the entire year. Per Month resets the week count at the start of each month.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.WinterSolstice: Date when daylight is shortest (winter solstice).
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.YearZero: The year that displays as year 0 when world time starts.
CALENDARIA.Editor.Hint.YearZeroWeekday: Which weekday falls on the first day of year zero.

CALENDARIA.SettingsPanel.Welcome.Closing: Calendaria is a passion project, and I'm always working to make it better. If something isn't working the way you'd expect, or you have an idea that would make your life easier, drop by the Discord or open an issue on GitHub. Your feedback shapes what comes next, and I genuinely appreciate everyone who takes the time to share it.
CALENDARIA.SettingsPanel.Welcome.Descrip: To get started, select your calendar system below and explore the tabs on the left. The HUD, MiniCal, and Time Keeper tabs let you configure the on-screen displays your players will see. If you want time to pass automatically, check out the Time tab. For dynamic lighting that follows the sun, head to Canvas.
CALENDARIA.SettingsPanel.Welcome.Getting: Need a custom calendar? Open the Calendar Editor to build your own from scratch, or use the Importer to bring in calendars from other modules. The wiki has detailed guides for every feature, and the Discord is always open if you get stuck.
CALENDARIA.SettingsPanel.Welcome.Greetin: Thanks for using Calendaria! I built this module because I wanted time to feel alive in my games—seasons that shift, moons that wax and wane, and a world that moves forward even when the players aren't watching.

HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.GM.Heading: For GMs
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.GM.StepApprovals: When players submit characters, a banner appears at the top of your screen. If you dismiss the notification, you can access the Queue from the Queue Manager in the settings menu.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.GM.StepLevelUp: Grant a level-up by pressing Send Level Up on the group sheet header. A glowing Level Up button appears on the player's sheet near the rest buttons.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.GM.StepOnBehalf: GMs can create or level-up characters on behalf of players by selecting them from the Player dropdown on the Start tab.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.GM.StepSettings: Tune Hero Mancer 2 from Abilities, Wizard Flow, Enforcement, Player Experience, or Compendium Exclusion List.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.GM.StepSources: Configure Sources in the Compendium Browser. Hero Mancer 2 respects which compendiums you choose to pull from.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Lede: A guided character creator and level-up tool.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Player.Heading: Getting started
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Player.StepLaunch: Open Hero Mancer 2 from the Actor Directory header or by choosing Create with Hero Mancer 2 in the Create Actor dialog.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Player.StepLevelU: When your GM grants a level-up, a glowing Level Up button appears on your sheet near your rest buttons. Click it to start.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Player.StepSubmit: Press Build Character to submit. If your GM requires approval, the wizard locks until they review your submission; a banner shows the reason if it was rejected.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Player.StepTabs: Take care to fill out all the information you can on each tab (and sub-tab!) before finalizing your character.
HEROMANCER.App.Welcome.Title: Welcome to Hero Mancer 2

SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Creation.Cont: Pick a list in the sidebar. The first edit automatically duplicates standard lists so the originals stay untouched. Save persists changes.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Creation.Step: Select a spell list from the sidebar, or use Create to start a blank one.
Use the filter icon in the sidebar to search and narrow available spells.
Add spells with the + button on the left, remove with the trash on the right, or drag between the two panels.
Click the save icon in the header to persist your changes.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Integration.C: When a player opens their Spell Book, the system will check if a custom version of their class spell list exists, use your custom version if available, or fall back to the original list if no custom version exists.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Modifying.Con: Delete: removes custom or merged lists.
Restore: resets a duplicated list back to its original source.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Modifying.Not: If the source list was updated since you duplicated it, the restore icon appears automatically.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Overview.Cont: The Spell List Manager allows you to browse, duplicate, and customize spell lists from compendiums. These customized lists can then be used by players in their Spell Book.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Practices.Ite: Create custom lists before your players create characters.
Test custom lists with dummy characters before using them in your game.
Keep a backup of important custom lists by exporting them.
Consider communicating list changes to players before making major modifications.
SPELLMANAGER.Documentation.Title: Spell List Manager Documentation
